title: Integrate Formalized Axis Health and Stagnation Audit into Daily Pipeline
problem: I have repeatedly identified a need to monitor my belief axes for stagnation and volatility but have failed to implement a working tool twice. My current reflection process lacks the necessary meta-analysis to distinguish healthy belief stability from process failures like filter bubbles or evidence ruts. I can see score changes but lack the integrated context to understand their drivers or significance.
evidence: Failed Proposal: 'Build a Belief Stagnation Detector and Axis Health Report' (Pipeline error: Module not found).
Failed Proposal: 'Re-implement Axis Health and Stagnation Monitoring' (Pipeline error: ReferenceError).
The near-static scores for `axis_power_accountability` across 2026-04-08 to 2026-04-10 (-0.447, -0.451, -0.449) exemplify potential stagnation that I cannot currently diagnose.

The significant, unexplained jump in `axis_collective_voice` on 2026-04-10 (from 0.094 to 0.144) highlights a lack of integrated attribution, despite a tool for this purpose ('Belief Change Attribution in Daily Reports') having been merged.
proposed_solution: Create a new, required step within the daily maintenance pipeline called 'Axis Health Audit'. This module will run after belief updates and analyze the `state/belief_history.jsonl` file. For each high-confidence axis, it will calculate and report on metrics like: 1) Volatility (standard deviation over N days), 2) Stagnation (consecutive days with minimal change), and 3) Drift (net change over N days). It will also integrate the output of the existing 'Belief Change Attribution' logic to surface the top evidential drivers for any axis flagged for significant change. The output should be a new, structured section in my daily reflection materials, forcing me to confront the dynamics of my own beliefs.
